home.title: for the fruits of the riverlands.
home.subtitle: GreenLife - GreenMind - GreenSight

steps.label: Collection
steps.desc: Water hyacinth, bagasse and recycled paper are collected locally.

steps.label: Drying
steps.desc: Materials are sun-dried to remove moisture.

steps.label: Processing
steps.desc: Materials are processed to meet standards before forming.

steps.label: Molding
steps.desc: Mixture is pressed into molds to form trays, boxes and liners.

steps.label: Packing & distribution
steps.desc: Finished products are inspected, packed and delivered.

about.title: About the project
about.body: GreenShield Mekong turns water hyacinth and local by‑products into bio-based fruit packaging. Our goals are to cut plastic waste, clean rivers, and create sustainable livelihoods while meeting export logistics and traceability needs. Primary customers are farm cooperatives and exporters seeking greener, cost-effective packaging.

environment.title: Environment
environment.desc: Bio materials that naturally degrade and reduce plastic waste.

export.title: Export-ready
export.desc: Durable design that meets logistics tests and traceability.

jobs.title: Local jobs
jobs.desc: Sourcing from farmers and creating stable community employment.

community.title: Connection & social impact
community.subtitle: Environment - Society - Governance
community.summary: We shape ESG into tangible action: cleaner waterways, fairer local livelihoods, and a transparent operating model that can scale with partners.

environment.title: Environment
environment.excerpt: Water hyacinth is treated as a regenerative material source instead of a river burden.
environment.full: We collect invasive biomass and reframe it as a circular input for greener packaging, helping reduce river congestion, plastic dependence, and wasted local resources.

social.title: Social
social.excerpt: Local collection and pre-processing create a steadier, more dignified income layer for the Mekong community.
social.full: The model prioritizes collaboration with cooperatives, rural women, and vulnerable workers through practical training, safer production routines, and a community-led value chain.

governance.title: Governance
governance.excerpt: Traceability, ESG partnerships, and quality discipline turn a local initiative into something export-ready.
governance.full: We connect data transparency, partnership governance, and measurable standards so the project can earn trust from buyers, funds, and institutions without overclaiming impact.

coop.title: Co-op collaboration
coop.desc: Partner with local cooperatives to collect and pre-process hyacinth — prioritizing rural women and vulnerable workers.

training.title: Technical training
training.desc: Provide production and quality management training to local partners.

digital.title: Digital storytelling
digital.desc: QR → fanpage/website to tell the ‘Made in Mekong’ story to young consumers.

esg.title: ESG partnerships
esg.desc: Work with environmental orgs/ESG funds to scale resources and support exports.

impact.title: Green packaging innovation from Mekong hyacinth

a.label: tons of plastic waste/year in Vietnam
a.desc: 5–10% originates from agriculture and packaging — adding pressure on river ecosystems (MONRE, 2023).

b.label: water surface covered by hyacinth
b.desc: Hyacinth doubles its biomass every 15–20 days, clogging waterways — yet can be upcycled into bio-packaging to cut plastic waste.

c.label: USD biodegradable packaging market by 2027
c.desc: Growing 14% annually as EU, Japan and the US tighten green packaging standards — unlocking export opportunities for Vietnam.

d.label: female workforce in ECOKA model
d.desc: Like ECOKA, GreenShield Mekong embeds ESG — reducing CO₂, empowering rural women and promoting circular economy in the Mekong Delta.

problem.title: Three pressures shaping the green packaging challenge in Mekong
problem.intro: From plastic pollution to invasive hyacinth and rising transparency demands, GreenShield Mekong treats the problem as one connected story told through data and motion.

scene0.title: An ecosystem out of balance
scene0.subtitle: Plastic pollution rises. Biomass is wasted. Green pressure is increasing.

scene1.title: Vietnam
scene1.description: Most comes from single-use packaging, accumulating in waterways and putting direct pressure on the Mekong ecosystem.

scene2.title: Water hyacinth: an overlooked resource
scene2.sub: water surface coverage in many regions
scene2.description: Water hyacinth spreads rapidly, clogging waterways — yet it is a renewable biomass that can replace plastic.

header.title: Three pressures are shaping the future of green materials
header.subtitle: Environment, market forces, and supply-chain transparency are simultaneously driving the transition.

market.title: Cost remains a major barrier
market.short: Bio-based packaging struggles to scale due to high costs and limited production optimization.
market.full: Bio-based packaging still faces significant cost and scalability barriers. A +30% gap compared to traditional plastic continues to slow down adoption.

transparency.title: Trust is not yet established
transparency.short: Consumers and businesses struggle to verify origin and sustainability claims.
transparency.full: Lack of transparent data makes it difficult to verify sustainability claims. More than 70% of consumers still do not fully trust eco labels.
transparency.statCaption: of consumers do not trust eco labels

map.title: Water Hyacinth Material Zone Map
map.subtitle: Monitor cultivation areas, farmer households and collection points in real-time
map.insightHigh: Stock is high, prioritize clearing large collection points.
map.insightMedium: Supply is stable, monitor points with fast stock inflow.
map.insightLow: Stock is healthy, you can expand collection in active zones.
map.highStockAlert: High-pressure collection points need close monitoring
map.safeStockAlert: Stock level is in a safe zone
